channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: NYT Strands #835 Hints and Answers for Today, June 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #835 for Tuesday, June 16, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

Before getting into the answers of today’s puzzle, here’s how you can have the cleanest reading experience for this and all of our stories: download the free Insider Gaming App for iOS or Android. 

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, June 16, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #835 are all words related to potential takeout items for lunch.

General Strands Hints for June 16

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: No __ for you.
  • Hint 2: A healthy option.
  • Hint 3: Turkey club, BLT, or meatball sub; potentially a hot dog.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #835 for June 16, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for June 16, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • CHAR
  • PAST
  • COST
  • WASP
  • WARS

Today’s Strands Answer for Game #835 on June 16, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • TACOS
  • SALAD
  • WRAP
  • RAMEN
  • SOUP
  • SANDWICH
  • GYRO
  • SPANAGRAM: WHATSFORLUNCH

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #835?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on June 17 will be Strands Game #836

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dead by Daylight Hits All-Time Player Count Peak 10 Years After Launch
item.content:encoded:
Dead by Daylight has just proven it has the staying power that trumps all comers in the horror space, hitting a new all-time high peak player count on Steam, a mighty ten years after launch. The game, which is one of the most beloved games in the business and has unbelievable energy right now, just underwent a huge anniversary update and received one of the most-wanted killers ever.

Today, on June 16, just over a decade to the day since Dead by Daylight was released, an all-new chapter surfaced introducing Jason Voorhees to the slasher-backed universe. The latest chapter has been incredibly well received by fans, and thanks to a massive sale, the game is doing better than ever.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

The New Dead by Daylight

Dead by Daylight won’t ever get a sequel; that much was confirmed recently by the team at Behaviour Interactive. But it doesn’t necessarily need one, if the latest numbers are anything to go by.

Now that Jason Voorhees’ chapter has landed in DBD, the game has skyrocketed up the charts, hitting an eye-watering peak player count on Steam of 125,820, edging out the previous record by a good five thousand players.

  • RELATED: Dead by Daylight Movie Has a New Director

Amazingly, Dead by Daylight has consistently done something that drastically few live service titles have managed to do over the years… It has gotten more popular with age.

In 2019, it hit a peak of around 75,000 players. Then, in 2021, around 105,000 players racked up the concurrency record. By 2025, DBD was looking at a peak of 120,000 players, and finally, today, it has climbed just beyond 125,000 concurrent users on Steam.

That’s a rare performance.

Jason Voorhees is arguably the most iconic horror character of all time (I might get in trouble for saying that), and his arrival in Dead by Daylight is easily what is driving up these numbers. It also helps that the game is currently 60% off on Steam, of course.

Have you jumped back into Dead by Daylight to experience Jason’s arrival? Let us know what you think about the update on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that XBOX is closing down Ninja Theory and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Five Pokémon Champions Beginner Tips for New Mobile Players
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Champions has made competitive battling much more accessible, especially as the game will be available on mobile phones.

When Pokémon Champions releases on Android and iOS, it will come with a big season update known as Regulation M-B.

It still hasn’t been revealed just exactly how many new Pokémon or items will be added to the game with the upcoming update.

But even if you don’t have a Switch or other Pokémon games to transfer Pokémon from through HOME, you can still compete.

Especially with these five tips to get you off to the perfect start and fully prepared for your own VGC battles.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android. 

  • Android Link
  • iOS Link

Complete the Tutorials for Big Rewards

It may seem obvious, but completing all the tutorials in Pokémon Champions is a must.

Not only will it teach you the basics of the game, it will also reward you heavily with a lot of free gifts.

Completing all the tutorials will give you a free Mega Stone to Mega Evolve a Pokémon, as well as VP, Quick Coupons, and Training Tickets.

VP (Victory Points) are the primary in-game resource used for recruiting mons, training them, and purchasing in-game held items.

Quick Coupons allow you to recruit (add a Pokémon to your roster) sooner for free without having to spend VP or wait 24 hours.

And Training Tickets will allow you to update your Pokémon’s moves, ability, nature, and stat boosts (formerly EVs) without spending VP.

Claim Free Gifts

There are a few free gifts you can claim to give yourself an extra boost at the start.

Firstly, just for logging in, you will receive Dragonite and Raichu as well as their Mega Stones for free. Make sure to claim them from the in-game mailbox.

Also, if you go to the submenu, then Mystery Gift, and enter the code ‘CHAMP10N‘ you’ll receive a free Machamp.

Finally, if you own Pokémon Legends Z-A, you can receive free gifts by transferring Pokémon from that game into Champions via Home.

Transferring over a Chestnaught, Delphox, Greninja, and Eternal Flower Floette originally caught in Legends Z-A will earn you the Mega Stones for all four Pokémon for free.

This is the only way to currently get Mega Floette, one of the strongest Pokémon, in Champions.

Before recruiting any Pokémon or building your teams, you can look at the strongest meta Pokémon in-game.

From the battle menu, scroll down to Battle Data, then select ranked battles from Season M-2.

Find the Pokémon section, select if you want to see data for single or double battles, then you can see the most popular Pokémon from the last season, as well as their most used moves, items, natures, and stat spread.

This is a great way to figure out how to build Pokémon for your own team and will give you ideas of who you might want to recruit.

  • Related News: Pokémon Champions producer teases legendaries and new features

Train Your Pokémon

When building your team, make sure you train your Pokémon to have the natures and stats you want.

Natures decrease one of your Pokémon’s stats and increase another. Most Pokémon either specialise in attack or special attack, so decreasing their weakest one is a common option.

These can be crucial in battle, earning you a little more damage or speed, which could win you a match.

And instead of the complex EVs and IVs system, Pokémon Champions allocates you 66 points to invest into each Pokémon’s stats.

For starters, just put the maximum 32 in two stats and the last two in something else. For example, Charizard is a fast special attacker, so 32 in special attack and speed plus two in defence is a common choice.

And make sure you use your Training Tickets to save VP, unless you’re only making one small change.

Learn The Types of Teams

Competitive Pokémon can be overwhelming at first, but learning about some of the team archetypes in Pokémon VGC is a good place to start.

However, selecting six strong Pokémon of different types, aka running a ‘Balance’ team, is also very viable.

Trick Room is a move that allows the slowest Pokémon to attack first for five turns. There are a lot of fun Trick Room teams to try in Pokémon Champions.

Otherwise, the main thing you need to worry about is weather. Weather typically gives a boost to one type of Pokémon and can lead to fun strategies.

For example, Solar Beam fires immediately in the sun, while Hurricane doesn’t miss when in the rain.

Although the new Mega Raichu abilities suggest that players may soon have to worry about Terrains, a similar battle system, as well.

Make sure to share your Pokémon Champions teams with our community on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Additionally, check out how to get additional Pokémon Champions team slots without spending money.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/five-pokemon-champions-beginner-tips-for-new-mobile-players/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Steam Next Fest: Demos From Insider Gaming Showcase Participants
item.content:encoded:
The latest Steam Next Fest is in full swing, and there are hundreds of great demos for you to check out and play. With so many demos, though, it can be hard to keep track and find the ones that stand out. That’s why we wanted to put together this list of all of the games that took part in May’s Insider Gaming Showcase that currently have demos available to play.

Before we get into it, be sure to download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news from Google Play or the App Store.

Insider Gaming Showcase Games At Steam Next Fest

Steam Next Fest runs until June 22 and allows you to try out new and upcoming games. At the Insider Gaming Showcase, we featured 55 games that are looking for funding or publisher support. Of those 55 titles, 19 are available for you to try now.

So, check out the games, give them a shot, and be sure to wishlist to let the developers know you care! You can also check out the details for each game as well.

DekaDuck – DEMO

DekaDuck is a 2D action platformer, featuring fully hand-drawn art and animation. Fight enemies using their own abilities and quirks to solve puzzles and defeat bosses, in a sci-fi world full of conspiracies!

ZeroSpace – DEMO

ZeroSpace is a sci-fi hybrid strategy game with a story-rich, party-based RPG campaign. As commander of the Protectorate forces, you must upgrade your mothership, build strong relationships with your crew and recruit a formidable army. Navigate the hard choices that will shape humanity’s future.

Smash ‘N Grab – DEMO

Smash ‘N Grab is a destructive, speedrunning beat ’em up starring Smash and Grab – a robot and raccoon duo pulling off explosive heists. Tethered by a rope, they race against the clock for the perfect score!

Brave New Wonders – DEMO

Command a tribe of intelligent automatons that can interpret ANY customized instruction you give in Brave New Wonders, a factory automation adventure. Pioneer a post-apocalyptic world, explore ruins from the past & discover a mysterious reality as you make your automated factories thrive!

Trickshotterz – DEMO

BEER PONG GETS GUNS… Only shots in the top count, pick abilities, grab wild powerups, and pull off absurd trickshots that hit harder the crazier they are. Play with up to 4 players locally, online, or hop into public matchmaking.

Meowstery Wisp – DEMO

A multiplayer social deduction and investigation game where players gather clues, debate, and manipulate trust to resolve a mystery; with no elimination until the big finale.

Don’t Lose Aggro – DEMO

Protect your team by forcing enemies to attack you in this single-player action roguelite inspired by MMO raids. Create unique builds, unlock powerful talents and master the art of tanking to command the chaos!

Dicetris – DEMO

A puzzle game where numbers matter. Dicetris fuses classic block-stacking with a fresh numerical twist: build columns of coloured dice and match the exact sum at the base to clear them. Use sharp maths and smarter strategy to stay ahead as you plan, stack, and clear your way through each challenge.

AGX GP – DEMO

AGX is a heart thumpingly fast, Anti-Gravity racing game. It’s lightning‑fast action and Xtreme ability‑based gameplay, will push your reactions and skills to the limit! Welcome To AGX!

Hawthorn – DEMO

Former developers from Bethesda, BioWare, and Naughty Dog bring you a sandbox RPG realm of anthropomorphic animals and fairy creatures. Play co-op or solo as you build a village of memorable NPCs, farm, craft, explore, and celebrate the seasons in an ever-changing world bigger than you.

Alea – DEMO

Intense, fast-paced arena battles with a high skill ceiling. Alea is a skill-first hero brawler with quick, high-pressure matches and a broad array of characters. Master two sides of every hero and forge a playstyle that is uniquely your own to outplay your rivals in 1v1, 2v2 and 3v3 matches.

Blind Descent – DEMO

Blind Descent is an open-world survival adventure that unveils the mysteries of the Martian depths. Play solo or with friends in 4-player co-op as you search for the missing team. Adapt to survive by building custom tools, upgrading the base, and/or mutating in this bizarre alien world.

Becoming Pablo – DEMO

Rule the underworld in this test of grand-strategy and city-building set in the 70’s-90’s drug trade. Conquer new territories, manage operations, and outsmart your rivals. Becoming Pablo isn’t just about building an empire – it’s about surviving it.

My Pet Hooligan – DEMO

Liberate your city from Zuck Corp in this 1-4 player co-op TPS with roguelite elements. Choose your arsenal of Gadgets, Buffs and cartoon weapons. Blast bots and skateboard as you regain your turf. Get stronger each zone on your way to beat the evil overlord MetaZuckBot! Ready to join the Rebellion?

Good News – DEMO

You’ve been hired as a chief editor to make this newspaper grow, but your writers are awful. Correct their drafts, set the tone of the news to fit your audience, make readership grow; and form a relationship with the shady politicians that will push you to give them good press in an electoral year

Zoominoes – DEMO

Build the ultimate Zoo in this number-go-up roguelike deckbuilder. Find powerful mythical animals, boost them with snacks, and even manipulate the board itself. Break the game by scoring millions (and more) with insane combos.

Island of Winds – DEMO

Island of Winds is an adventure game set in a fantastical world inspired by 17th century Iceland and folklore. Experience the story of Brynhildur, a Balance Keeper who embarks on a journey of self-discovery. Core gameplay involves intriguing puzzles, spellcasting, and a focus on empathy encounters.

Ungaland – DEMO

Dive into a prehistoric world of adventure where the power of primal seeds changes everything. Unlock abilities, attach them to your weapons, and face challenges in a vibrant land full of action, and surprises.

Eclipse 2: Invasion of Darkness – DEMO

Eclipse 2: Invasion of Darkness – is an isometric, story-driven game in the Action RPG genre, combining the mechanics of metroidvania exploration and the dynamics of modern slashers. Lead the medieval resistance against the alien invasion on Earth!

In other news, read about showcase participant Smash ‘N Grab being nominated for an award this month. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android
